title: pholidia drummondii
intro: Pholidia drummondii, also known as the Drummond's pholidia, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It is a shrub that belongs to the family Rubiaceae and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Pholidia drummondii are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
